messages.content: It boots me out — logs me out — every time I open the remote tab, but I stay logged in everywhere else.

<think>
Staying logged in everywhere except the remote tab is the key contrast. A general session failure would log them out across the app; a cache would show stale data, not eject them. Being kicked at exactly one gate is the token being rejected there — expired or missing the claim that gate checks. So it's a token issue at that feature, not a cache or a broad logout.
</think>
{"diagnosis": {"leading_root_cause": "TOKEN_SCOPE", "confidence": 0.7, "differential": [{"rc": "TOKEN_SCOPE", "likelihood": "high", "why": "ejected at one gate while authenticated elsewhere = token refused there"}, {"rc": "ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE", "likelihood": "low", "why": "a cache doesn't sign you out of a tab"}], "evidence_in_ticket": ["logs me out", "open the remote tab", "stay logged in everywhere else"], "to_confirm": ["whether the entitlement is active for the account", "whether the token carries the feature scope or is expired"], "sub_cause": "token_expired"}, "runbook_id": "RB-IAM-03", "why_plain": "You do have the subscription, but your sign-in session did not pick up the new permission yet, so the app blocks the feature. Signing out and back in usually refreshes it.", "why_technical": "the session token has expired and the app is not refreshing it, so the feature endpoint returns 403.", "owner_team": "HG Identity Team", "support_contact": "HG Identity on-call (Slack #iam-oncall)", "escalation": "HG L2 if the entitlement-to-scope mapping is wrong (needs a reissue)", "fix_steps": ["Confirm the entitlement is active", "Confirm the token is expired", "Re-authenticate / clear the session", "Verify"], "eta_ttr": "Under 1 hour; immediate after re-auth", "severity": "S3", "priority": "P2", "churn_risk": {"level": "low-medium", "why": "a permission error feels broken, but the fix is fast once the token is refreshed"}, "compensation": {"offer": "small goodwill gesture only if it recurs", "proactive": false, "note": "usually none; not offered proactively"}, "similar_incident": "INC-0923", "customer_self_service": [{"tier": 1, "action": "Sign out of the app and sign back in — this refreshes your permissions and usually clears the error.", "addresses": "TOKEN_SCOPE", "expected_time": "~2 min", "verify": "the permission error clears", "if_fails": "go to step 2"}, {"tier": 2, "action": "Force-close and reopen the app; if needed, reinstall it and sign in again.", "addresses": "TOKEN_SCOPE", "expected_time": "~5 min", "verify": "the feature opens", "if_fails": "go to step 3"}, {"tier": 3, "action": "Make sure you are signed into the same account that purchased the feature (check the email on the account).", "addresses": "TOKEN_SCOPE", "expected_time": "~2 min", "verify": "you are on the correct account", "if_fails": "go to step 4"}, {"tier": 4, "action": "Note the exact error (e.g. a 403 or permission message) so support can pinpoint it.", "addresses": "general", "expected_time": "~1 min", "verify": "you have the error details", "if_fails": "go to step 5"}, {"tier": 5, "action": "If a fresh sign-in does not help, contact support to reset your session.", "addresses": "escalate", "expected_time": "handled by support (<1h)", "verify": "a specialist refreshes your access", "if_fails": "a specialist takes over"}], "artifacts": {"rca_md": "## Root Cause Analysis
**Leading cause:** TOKEN_SCOPE (RB-IAM-03)

**Why:** the entitlement is active, but the IAM/HIDAS token was issued without the entitlement's scope (stale or expired), so the feature endpoint returns 403 until the token/scope is refreshed.

**Evidence from ticket:** logs me out; open the remote tab; stay logged in everywhere else

**Owner:** HG Identity Team · **Severity:** S3 · **Priority:** P2", "work_order_md": "## Work Order
- Owner: HG Identity Team
- Support: HG Identity on-call (Slack #iam-oncall)
- Escalation: HG L2 if the entitlement-to-scope mapping is wrong (needs a reissue)
- ETA: Under 1 hour; immediate after a token refresh in most cases

**Steps:**
1. Confirm the entitlement is active for the account
2. Check whether the token carries the feature scope
3. Force a token/scope refresh (re-authenticate)
4. If the scope mapping is wrong, fix it and reissue the token
5. Verify the feature no longer returns 403", "customer_email": "Hi, thanks for reaching out. You do have the subscription, but your sign-in session did not pick up the new permission yet, so the app blocks the feature. Signing out and back in usually refreshes it. Please sign out and back in to refresh your permissions; if the feature still shows a permission error, we will refresh it on our side. I'll follow up to confirm everything is working.", "diagram_mermaid": "sequenceDiagram
  participant E as Entitlement
  participant I as IAM/HIDAS
  participant A as App
  E->>I: entitlement active
  Note over I: token/scope not refreshed -> missing feature scope
  A-->>A: feature returns 403 / permission denied"}}
I paid and it doesn't work — keeps asking me to subscribe, CR-V Touring.

<think>
Past the generic line, the cue is subscribe prompts on a CR-V Touring. Never-granted, the trim missing from the matrix. So an eligibility gap.
</think>

**Why:** payment.succeeded was delivered, but the eligibility check rejected the region/trim/plan combo, so no entitlement record was created; nothing propagates to IAM/CCS/TCU.

**Owner:** Entitlement Platform Team · **Severity:** S3 · **Priority:** P2", "work_order_md": "## Work Order
- Owner: Entitlement Platform Team
- Support: DSD Entitlement on-call (Slack #ent-oncall)

- ETA: Typically 2-3 hours (median ~140 min) for a matrix config change plus replay; up to one business day if a code deploy is required

  A-->>A: keeps prompting Subscribe"}}
Just purchased Remote Climate a couple minutes ago, it's not on yet. Should I be worried?

<think>
A couple of minutes in and asking whether to worry — no, not yet. That's well inside the window where activation simply hasn't propagated. Nothing here suggests a real failure: no long wait, no charge problem, no error. So the reasonable read is a brief delay that should clear on its own shortly.
</think>

App keeps prompting Subscribe even though I paid. It's a CR-V Touring. Persistent since I bought it.

<think>
Paid but persistently re-prompted to subscribe, with nothing active — that's a never-granted state, not a cached one, since a cache wouldn't hold across every surface this long. The specific detail they give is the trim, a CR-V Touring. When one particular trim won't activate, the likely cause is that combination missing from the eligibility matrix, so the rule rejects it. Not the car or a stale view — an eligibility gap.
</think>

<think>
